title: record-fields runtime reflection — needs a record-type -> field-name registry
goal: lib/core/reflection.esk documents `record-fields` as DEFERRED: R7RS records expand at parse time into a vector with a type-tag in slot 0 plus accessor/mutator definitions, but the field *names* exist only in the parser/codegen at expansion time and are never embedded in the runtime value. `(record-fields some-record)` therefore cannot recover field names today without a separate runtime registry. The file already contains a full implementation plan (see the comment block at the bottom of lib/core/reflection.esk): generate a global association list at expansion time of `((record-type-tag . (field1 field2 ...)) ...)`, then have `(record-fields obj)` extract slot 0 and look it up. Estimated effort noted inline as ~1 day in lib/frontend/parser.cpp plus a small runtime helper.
next_actions: Implement the expansion-time record-type -> field-name registry in lib/frontend/parser.cpp per the plan already documented in lib/core/reflection.esk.
Wire (record-fields obj) to look up the type tag in that registry.
Add tests/features/record_fields_test.esk covering a simple record, a record with zero extra fields, and (record-fields on-a-non-record) error behavior.
acceptance: (record-fields some-record) returns the field names in declaration order for any define-record-type-defined record.
Calling record-fields on a non-record value raises a clear type error, not a crash.
No change to existing record codegen size/perf for programs that never call record-fields.
ux_payoff: Completes the reflection triad (procedure-arity / describe / record-fields) that lib/core/reflection.esk was built around; low urgency since describe already covers most debugging use cases.
